--- SOURCE 1: Professional Development for Educators & Teachers - Michigan Virtual ---

SUMMARY:
### Online Student Courses → ### Professional Development for Educators → ### About Michigan Virtual → Your trusted source for K-12 online learning insights, teaching strategies, and success stories from Michigan’s leading virtual education provider. ## We’ve provided professional development to Michigan educators for nearly 20 years! Our Professional Learning Services team collaborates with subject-matter experts to develop timely, comprehensive, and action-oriented learning opportunities for teachers, administrators, mentors, and counselors. *Michigan Virtual* has designed a variety of free and low-cost PD courses on online and blended learning to help you get started. First, your Michigan Professional Teaching Certificate expires after five years, and renewal requires 150 hours of education-related professional learning. Access our Professional Learning Portal to view more than 250 courses.

# Professional Learning Services

## A flexible online community for Michigan educators

Your trusted source for K-12 online learning insights, teaching strategies, and success stories from Michigan’s leading virtual education provider. Join us as we explore innovative approaches to help students thrive in the digital age.

## We’ve provided professional development to Michigan educators for nearly 20 years!

Our Professional Learning Services team collaborates with subject-matter experts to develop timely, comprehensive, and action-oriented learning opportunities for teachers, administrators, mentors, and counselors.

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/02/Professional-develoment-Michigan-Virtual-1024x683.jpg)

## Popular Services & Courses

![desks in classroom](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![desks in classroom](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/desks-in-classroom-1024x638.jpg)

## Hybrid & Online Teaching

Unsure where to start with online or blended teaching? Have some ideas, but need more resources? *Michigan Virtual* has designed a variety of free and low-cost PD courses on online and blended learning to help you get started.

![Books in a library](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![Books in a library](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2017/03/jessica-ruscello-196422-1024x683.jpg)

### TV Series & Book Studies

Where innovation and inspiration transform your practice as you watch or read content that’s relevant to you.

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/10/769-1024x682.jpg)

### Group Registration Enrollments

Team up with Michigan Virtual to build your one-of-a-kind professional development experience.

![keyboard with mini graduation cap, diploma, and ladder leaned against book](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![keyboard with mini graduation cap, diploma, and ladder leaned against book](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/online-college-1024x684.jpg)

### Micro-Credentials

Take your learning to the next level by applying your knowledge to your own practice.

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/862-1024x683.jpg)

### Collaboratives

Local teachers instruct local students with Michigan Virtual’s award-winning content.

![Red blood cells in travel an artery](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![Red blood cells in travel an artery](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/07/iStock-1005715630-1024x576.jpg)

### State Required Trainings

Complete required training at your own pace, any time, anywhere.

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/iStock-2130240743-1024x683.jpg)

### Workshops

Looking to host a workshop focused on AI or implementing blended learning in the classroom? Here are some options for you that can be customized to fit your timing needs.

![People working together over table of papers and sticky notes, only their hands are visible.](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![People working together over table of papers and sticky notes, only their hands are visible.](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/iStock-2073485467-1-1024x683.jpg)

### Instructional Product Development

At Michigan Virtual, we focus on creating impactful, accessible, and results-driven learning experiences using our research-backed 5D Process.

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/iStock-2153831641.jpg)

### MV DeliverED

Say hello to your personalized professional development solution and summer learning adventure delivered straight to your door!

![Female Asian teacher working at desk in classroom.](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![Female Asian teacher working at desk in classroom.](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/iStock-515264060-1024x683.jpg)

### Teacher Resources

Whether you’re looking for fresh perspectives, new-to-you ideas, or thoughtful strategies, you’ll find a rich mix of resources here to help you do what you do best!

Our team of Professional Learning Specialists has curated some of their favorite resources to foster creativity and innovation that can be directly applied to your classroom.

## Take one of *Michigan Virtual*'s 250+ SCECH-bearing PD courses and let us do the SCECH processing!

Last year, we processed more than 300,000 SCECHs!

### District-provided professional development​

Administrators often want to provide school- or district-wide professional development, and we are here to help. Through our Group Registration and Enrollment program, you can choose courses for your staff to complete or curate a catalog with courses for staff to choose. Additionally, we offer personalized coaching services for blended and online learning efforts, as well as face-to-face synchronous training.

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/iStock-956725740-1024x683.jpg)
![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/610-1024x683.jpg)

### Professional development for mentors

Mentors play a critical role in supporting students but often do not receive as much training as they would like to perform this significant role. In addition to mentor resources such as our comprehensive [mentor guide](https://michiganvirtual.org/resources/guides/mentor-guide/), mentors can receive a [free online learning review](https://michiganvirtual.org/research/online-program-reviews/) and attend local [mentor meetups](https://michiganvirtual.org/professionals/mentors/meetups/).

### Professional development for counselors

From college counseling to military counseling and career exploration, we offer several PD courses specifically for counselors. Discover how to develop relationships with college admissions officers, understand military offerings for high school graduates, and guide students to be career-ready after graduation.

![](data:image/gif;base64,R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///yH5BAEAAAAALAAAAAABAAEAAAIBRAA7)
![](https://michiganvirtual.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/iStock-1155974947-1024x683.jpg)

## Don’t take our word for it!

##### Hear what Michigan teachers have said about our products/services:

“I love the tips that each section gave so you could see how improving in that area is simpler than maybe we thought it would be.”

“[This course] did what we should all do in our classroom: Start the process and show students how to carry that forward into the future.”

“I would like to thank you and all of your partners who made these courses available to me as an educator. I appreciate the time and effort that was put into this great project. Thanks again for all your support in the world of virtual learning.”

### High Quality Resources & Learning from Michigan's Best

Expand your professional growth through our curated collection of training courses, delivered in partnership with leading educational organizations.

### Office of Great Start

### Frequently asked questions

You receive your Certificate of Completion within 12 hours of fully completing the course. You will receive your SCECH credits within 10 full business days of receiving your Certificate of Completion.

There are two reasons. First, your Michigan Professional Teaching Certificate expires after five years, and renewal requires 150 hours of education-related professional learning. Second, quality PD courses can help you master not only useful professional and personal skills but also aid in improving student outcomes.

Access our Professional Learning Portal to view more than 250 courses. Select a course and click “Enroll in this course.”

We offer dozens of free, SCECH-bearing courses. The cost for individual courses is always listed and can range from free to around $200 based on the number of hours and SCECHs.

Contact our Customer Care Team with any questions or concerns you might have. They are available Monday through Friday, 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. EST.

The GRE program is highly customizable and can include nearly every course we offer. Explore our District-Provided Professional Development page for more information.

## Interested in learning more?

Please fill out the form below if you are interested in speaking to one of our professional development specialists about providing educators in your district with goal-focused professional learning opportunities.

--- SOURCE 2: 5 Professional Development Opportunities for Virtual Teachers ---
URL: https://www.elevatek12.com/blog/elevate-in-action/virtual-professional-development-for-teachers/

SUMMARY:
Previous workshops have covered course design, best practices for online learning, and using technology effectively in the virtual classroom to drive student success. SimpleK12 hosts webinars, workshops, and online professional development courses for K-12 teachers. Many virtual K-12 teachers take self-paced online courses and participate in professional learning communities (PLCs). Best Practices for Virtual Professional Development for Teachers Many teachers have their own unique learning goals and objectives for their virtual professional development. Just like they emphasize in their own classrooms, virtual teachers must actively participate in their professional development sessions. Empower Virtual Teachers Through Unique Professional Development Opportunities Professional development is the key that unlocks a virtual teacher’s potential so they can create thriving learning communities.

FULL CONTENT:
Few educators could have predicted the widespread adoption of remote learning. Then came the COVID-19 pandemic, which forced teachers to adapt quickly to a [virtual learning environment](https://www.elevatek12.com/blog/elevate-in-action/best-online-teaching-platforms-for-k-12/).
By the end of 2020, K-12 schools worldwide [received 51 million tablets and laptops](https://www.mobileguardian.com/increased-prevalence-of-mobile-devices-in-k-12-schools/).
With the soaring popularity of remote and hybrid learning, virtual professional development is vital. It helps teachers understand the new tools of the trade, such as video conferencing, learning management systems, and online collaboration tools.
In addition, it helps virtual teachers master new pedagogical approaches and strategies for engaging students remotely.
Fortunately, educators have many options for expanding their skills in the online learning environment. These are a few of our favorite professional growth opportunities for virtual teachers.
1. Webinars and Online Workshops
--------------------------------
Webinars and virtual workshops offer a convenient, accessible way for educators to grow their skills and stay current with the latest teaching methods.
Many educational organizations, professional associations, and ed-tech companies host live and [recorded webinars](https://www.elevatek12.com/blog/webinar/) and online workshops. Topics range from creating interactive activities to blended learning practices.
The beauty of webinars and online workshops is their flexibility. Teachers can participate in live sessions or access recorded content at their convenience. We’ve included a few examples below to get you started.
\* \*\*Quality Matters:\*\*[Quality Matters](https://www.qualitymatters.org/professional-development/workshops) offers online workshops designed specifically for K-12 teachers new to online teaching. Previous workshops have covered course design, best practices for online learning, and using technology effectively in the virtual classroom to [drive student success](https://www.elevatek12.com/blog/elevate-in-action/student-success/).
The workshops are reasonably priced, and with a Quality Matters membership, you receive a special discount on professional development.
\* \*\*SimpleK12:\*\*Teachers can access various free and paid professional development opportunities through [SimpleK12](https://www.teachersoftomorrow.org/school-districts/simplek12/). SimpleK12 hosts webinars, workshops, and online professional development courses for K-12 teachers. Topics include curriculum development, classroom management, and instructional technology, among many others.
\* \*\*SchoolsFirst FCU Center for Creativity and Critical Thinking:\*\*Through Cal State Fullerton’s SchoolsFirst FCU Center for Creativity and Critical Thinking, teachers can participate in [free webinars on K-12 online teaching](https://ed.fullerton.edu/sfccct/news/K12OnlineTeaching.php).
The webinars explore new strategies and techniques for teaching online. During each session, teachers will have dedicated planning time to develop curriculum or action plans for their classrooms.
2. Virtual Coaching and Mentoring
---------------------------------
Virtual coaching and mentoring are great ways to supplement the knowledge gained from webinars and workshops. They allow virtual teachers to refine their craft in engaging one-on-one or small group sessions with experienced mentors or instructional coaches.
Through coaching and mentoring, teachers receive personalized support and feedback tailored to their needs. They can then implement that feedback directly in their online instruction to boost their effectiveness.
If you’re looking for the right coach or mentor, we’ve included a few options below.
\* \*\*School or District Programs:\*\*Many schools and districts provide virtual coaching and mentoring programs for teachers. Check with your school administration to see if your school or district offers these types of opportunities. If you’re an administration, consider implementing a mentorship program in your school district to connect new educators with seasoned pros.
\* \*\*External Organizations:\*\*Organizations like [Sibme Virtual Coaches](https://www.sibme.com/services/virtual-coaching/teachers), [Modern Classrooms Project](https://www.modernclassrooms.org/mentorship), and [EdConnective Teach](https://www.edconnective.com/virtual-coaching) specialize in mentoring and coaching virtual teachers. Teachers can connect with other experienced educators and mentors for ongoing support and feedback.
3. Online Courses and Professional Learning Communities
-------------------------------------------------------
If you prefer to explore a specific topic in depth, online courses can be a great option. Many virtual K-12 teachers take self-paced online courses and participate in professional learning communities (PLCs).
Online PLCs connect educators across schools and school districts, helping them continuously enhance their skills. Participants can share best practices, troubleshoot challenges, and learn from each other’s experiences. PLCs are also terrific for networking with other educators who share your interests and goals.
To find online courses in specific areas of teaching and learning, explore platforms like [Coursera](https://www.coursera.org/), [edX](https://www.edx.org/), and [Teachable](https://teachable.com/industry/academics-and-languages). These platforms offer a range of courses, from fostering social-emotional learning in a virtual environment to nurturing student achievement. Most courses give learners the option to go at their own pace.
\* \*\*edWeb:\*\*[edWeb](https://home.edweb.net/professional-learning-communities-with-free-webinars/) is a free professional learning community that connects educators interested in a wide range of topics. Teachers can join a PLC focused on virtual teaching or a community related to their teaching areas, such as PreK-3 Digital Learning or Inspire a Love of Reading.
\* \*\*Association for Supervision and Curriculum Development (ASCD):\*\*[ASCD](https://www.ascd.org/communities) connects educators virtually based on their shared interests. Within this digital community, virtual teachers can meet and collaborate with like-minded educators on a variety of K-12 education topics.
4. Virtual Conferences and Networking Events
--------------------------------------------
Even if virtual teachers can’t attend an event in person, there are many conferences and events they can join remotely.
These virtual conferences, networking events, and symposiums give educators a dynamic space to network with their fellow educators. Attendees can share inspiring ideas and stay up-to-date on the latest advancements in virtual education.
You can find information on virtual conferences and networking events through professional organizations, universities, and education companies. Virtual conference platforms like Zoom and Microsoft Teams also have event calendars where you can search for conferences using keywords like “K-12” or “remote learning.”
Once you find an event to attend, you can register for the sessions that reflect your professional interests. This way, you won’t have to sit through sessions that aren’t relevant to your subject area or the grade you teach.
Here are some resources to explore:
\* \*\*National Educational Technology Conference (NETC):\*\*Hosted by the [International Society for Technology in Education](https://iste.org/) (ISTE), this annual virtual conference includes workshops, sessions, and networking opportunities for educators of all grade levels. Sessions cover topics like artificial intelligence and project-based learning for self-directed learners.
\* \*\*Aurora Institute Virtual Events:\*\*[The Aurora Institute](https://aurora-institute.org/events-webinars/) (formerly known as the International Association for K-12 Online Learning or iNACOL) regularly hosts online conferences, webinars, and symposiums on all aspects of K-12 education. Administrators, educators, lawmakers, and researchers come together to present and discuss some of the most challenging topics in education.
5. Self-Directed Online Learning and Resources
----------------------------------------------
For virtual teachers who prefer to learn at their own pace, resources like blogs, podcasts, videos, [books](https://www.elevatek12.com/blog/inside-elevate/six-professional-development-books-our-teachers-are-reading/), and research articles offer invaluable learning opportunities. Teachers can explore topics of interest at a pace that fits their needs and schedule.
Following trusted online resources like education blogs and podcasts is a good way to stay informed. Many teachers set aside time in their day or week for professional development. During this time, they can expand their knowledge and explore topics that are relevant to their teaching practice and professional goals.
Check out these resources for self-directed learning:
\* \*\*Edutopia:\*\*[Edutopia](https://www.edutopia.org/) is a comprehensive resource for educators. It provides information, inspiration, and practical strategies for learning and teaching preK-12 education.
\* \*\*Podcasts:\*\*Podcast listeners have many great education-related options. [\_The Cult of Pedagogy Podcast\_](https://www.cultofpedagogy.com/pod/), [\_House of #EdTech\_](https://www.chrisnesi.com/), the [\_10 Minute Teacher Podcast\_](https://www.coolcatteacher.com/10-minute-teacher-podcast/), and [\_Teach Me, Teacher\_](https://teachmeteacherpodcast.com/) are some popular education podcasts for teachers.
Best Practices for Virtual Professional Development for Teachers
----------------------------------------------------------------
Virtual learning is constantly evolving. One-size-fits-all continuing education workshops and pedagogical approaches no longer enable teachers to succeed in the virtual classroom.
Here are a few best practices to help you make the most of professional development opportunities.
### Understand the Goals First
Many teachers have their own unique learning goals and objectives for their virtual professional development. Maybe they want to become more comfortable with digital learning tools or facilitate more engaging discussions with students.
So it’s important to clearly identify your goals so you can map out your professional development plan effectively. From there, you can decide what types of opportunities will put you on the right path.
### Actively Participate
Just like they emphasize in their own classrooms, virtual teachers must actively participate in their professional development sessions. This means asking questions, sharing insights, and collaborating with their peers.
Active participation leads to better learning outcomes and creates meaningful dialogue between educators. Some teachers even create “pods” with other educators to experiment with new strategies in their classrooms. They report back to share what worked and what didn’t work so they can learn from each other’s experiences.
### Implement New Ideas and Learnings
Of course, professional development doesn’t do much good if you don’t use the teaching strategies and techniques you’ve learned in your teaching practices and lesson plans.
For example, if you’ve been learning about integrating hands-on learning activities into the virtual classroom, you could experiment with a new classroom polling technology to engage students.
### Reflect on Professional Growth
As you expand your knowledge and put new skills into practice, don’t forget to take some time for self-reflection. Think about how far you’ve come, identify areas for continued improvement, and celebrate your successes. These insights will guide your ongoing professional development journey.
Empower Virtual Teachers Through Unique Professional Development Opportunities
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Mastering the virtual classroom and creating positive learning experiences for students requires a unique skill set. Professional development is the key that unlocks a virtual teacher’s potential so they can create thriving learning communities.
When teachers have the right professional development tools, every learner can have access to the high-quality teaching they deserve. That’s why Elevate K-12 is committed to supporting not just our educators but all teachers in their professional growth and development journeys.
\* \* \*

--- SOURCE 3: Online Learning & Professional Development – CLI Engage Public ---
URL: https://public.cliengage.org/tools/online-learning/

SUMMARY:
CLI Engage hosts many online professional development courses for early childhood teachers, administrators, coaches, and parents.

FULL CONTENT:
Menu

![CLI Engage Logo 20](https://public.cliengage.org/wp-content/uploads/sites/10/2017/06/cli-engage-logo-bp-20.png)

# Online Learning & Professional Development

![HERO-icons-purple](https://cli-main.org/public/wp-content/uploads/sites/10/2017/06/CLI-ENGAGE-new-HERO-icons-purple-300x300.png)CLI Engage hosts many online professional development courses for early childhood teachers, administrators, coaches, and parents. After completing an online course on CLI Engage, users can receive a certificate certifying their completion for either Continuing Professional Education hours (CPE) or Child Care Licensing clock hours. Some courses also provide opportunities to purchase  continuing education units (CEU). If users also have an account on the [Texas Workforce Registry](https://public.cliengage.org/tools/quality/texas-workforce-registry/), part of the Texas Early Childhood Professional Development System (TECPDS), the certificates automatically transfer to their personal account.

![HERO-icons-purple](https://cli-main.org/public/wp-content/uploads/sites/10/2017/06/CLI-ENGAGE-new-HERO-icons-purple-300x300.png)

Our resources are available at no cost to many programs in Texas. [LEARN MORE ABOUT ELIGIBILITY](https://public.cliengage.org/about/eligibility/)

### COURSES

##### **eCircle Professional Development**

**16-Course Series  
Best Fit for: PreK, K, 1st**

eCircle represents more than 70 hours of online courses featuring extensive video-based demonstrations of effective instructional practices, as well as application-based assignments and activities.

##### **Circle Infant & Toddler Teacher Training: “Play with Me” Series**

**7-Course Series  
Best Fit for: Birth to 3**

Learn strategies to target infant and toddler language, literacy, social-emotional, cognitive, and physical development, as well as best practices in responsive caregiving and using developmental assessments.

##### **Beginning Education: Early Childcare at Home**

**5-Course Series  
Best Fit for: Birth to 5**

BEECH is designed for home-based childcare providers of children from birth to five and focuses on supporting children's social-emotional, cognitive, language, literacy, and mathematics development.

##### **Texas Prekindergarten Guidelines Training**

**10-Course Series  
Best Fit for: PreK**

CLI partnered with the Texas Education Agency for this online training that guides teachers through the child outcomes and instructional strategies presented in the Texas Prekindergarten Guidelines (2015).

##### **Infant, Toddler, & Three- Year-Old Guidelines**

**5-Course Series  
Best Fit for: Birth to 3**

This series familiarizes caregivers with the state’s early learning guidelines across four domains: language and communication, health and motor skills, social and emotional development, and cognitive development.

##### **Core Competencies for Early Childhood**

**2-Course Series  
Best Fit for: Birth to 5**

This training introduces learners to the TX Core Competencies for Early Childhood Practitioners and Administrators, developed by the TX Head Start State Collaboration Office and TX Early Learning Council.

##### **Circle CDA Training Program**

**More than 120 Hours Available  
Best Fit for: PreK**

The goal of the Circle CDA Training Program is to provide all 120 hours of high quality, formal professional development hours to enable early childhood teachers earn the required training hours needed to apply for the Preschool Child Development Associate© (CDA) national credential.

##### **ECE Professional Micro-Credentials**

**3 Micro-credentials  
Best Fit for: ECE Professionals**

Micro-credentials provide professionals with professional development acknowledgement for competencies developed throughout their career. Each micro-credential is comprised of badges that are linked to specific, demonstrable skills or competencies.

##### **Workforce Registry Accounts for Texas Rising Star**

**2-Course Series  
Best Fit for: TRS Providers**

“Unlocking TECPDS: Making the Most of Your Workforce Registry Account for Texas Rising Star” are a series of courses developed to provide Texas Rising Star (TRS) Providers and Staff with information about recent changes to TECPDS and its Texas Workforce Registry.

##### **Family Engagement Professional Development**

**4-Course Series  
Best Fit for: Birth to K**

In this series of online trainings, you’ll learn about research-driven ways to partner with families in supporting children’s development using resources that are freely available to school districts and families.

### VIDEO SERIES

##### **Understanding Developmental Screening & Early Intervention**

**10 Videos  
Best Fit for: Parents and Families**

This video series is designed help families understand the purposes of developmental screening and how to use Early Childhood Intervention services for further assessment and intervention when needed.

FULL CONTENT:
![ACT Now IL logo in full color](https://actnowillinois.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/act-now-il-logo-full-color.svg)

### Professional Development

Running an Afterschool Program

Safety, Health, & Nutrition

Family & Community

![ACT Now IL logo in full color](https://actnowillinois.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/act-now-il-logo-full-color.svg)

# Remote Professional Development Opportunities

## Afterschool Alliance

Afterschool Alliance hosts FREE online webinars for OST providers on a variety of topics. Click [here](http://www.afterschoolalliance.org/webinars.cfm) to review the upcoming events and access the recorded sessions.

## The National Afterschool Association

The National Afterschool Association, through TurnkeyPD, is offering a series of professional development resources designed to help executives and direct providers build specific [core knowledge and competencies](https://naaweb.org/resources/core-competencies) of afterschool and youth development professionals. Click [here](https://naaweb.org/resources/turnkeypd) to review the PD materials.

## [Foundations Inc.](https://foundationsinc.org/?eType=EmailBlastContent&eId=97d57d39-14f5-4410-9e9a-f033c004c3b5)

[Foundations Inc.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T31a7b341-2cb2-4060-a634-560c15dde47c/7c3f5477-d919-4665-9c9b-1b3686a6d453) is providing limited access to their FREE and engaging professional development mini-series for afterschool providers. Participants will receive an e-certificate after completing the sessions. To learn more about the series, click [here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T671602af-b27b-4c28-b03b-7f0b49eb79ac/7c3f5477-d919-4665-9c9b-1b3686a6d453).

## The Atlanta Speech School’s Cox Campus

The Atlanta Speech School’s Cox Campus is providing FREE video-based professional development courses and at-home learning resources for afteschool providers. These resources are geared towards providers that teach students between the ages of birth to 9, and cover a wide variety of topics. Click [here](https://www.coxcampus.org/) to review the online materials.

## The U.S. Department of Education

The U.S. Department of Education, through its You for Youth program, has 15 FREE courses for 21st Century providers and afterschool program facilitators. Click [here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc531ae56-9212-43e3-9d48-54fc8513ff04/7c3f5477-d919-4665-9c9b-1b3686a6d453) to access these online materials. Upon completion, participants are eligible to receive certificates.

## NextWaveSTEM

NextWaveSTEM is providing [FREE Professional Development Trainings](https://nextwavestem.com/pages/professional-development?eType=EmailBlastContent&eId=0744d8f4-80fb-4e75-95cc-5ab4d0bdd462) for afterschool providers and educators. Topics include how to effectively teach remotely during the COVID-19 pandemic. For educators in Illinois, NextWaveSTEM is offering the following: After completing each one-hour webinar, you will receive one ISBE professional development hour – sent via email!

## **Penn State Extension’s Better Kid Care**

Penn State Extension’s Better Kid Care program offers [low-cost and remote learning professional development opportunities for OST providers.](https://extension.psu.edu/programs/betterkidcare) Topics focus on positive youth outcomes.

## Teach Illinois

Teach Illinois is offering [FREE professional development webinars and resources for teachers making the transition to online education in Illinois.](http://www.teachillinois.com/) The webpage hosts online courses, book studies, webinars, podcasts, online administrator programs approved by ISBE, and other professional development materials.

## Click2SciencePD

Click2SciencePD offers [professional development resources for STEM program facilitators and afterschool providers.](http://www.click2sciencepd.org/) The organization’s resources are low-cost opportunities and their web lessons cost $5 for each professional development credit.

## The IRIS Center at Vanderbilt University

The IRIS Center at Vanderbilt University has [professional development opportunities with a focus on supporting students with special abilities](https://iris.peabody.vanderbilt.edu/).

## Recent Tweets from [@ACTNowCoalition](https://twitter.com/ACTNowCoalition)

![](https://actnowillinois.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/homepage-twitter-placeholder.jpg)

Sign up for our newsletter to get resources and updates every Tuesday morning.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Comprehensive online courses and workshops equip educators with necessary skills for remote teaching, focusing on effective strategies, technology integration, and student engagement.
Facilitates collaborative learning between educators, fostering support and feedback through shared experiences, enhancing instructional practices, and building a sense of community.
Provides educators with curated teaching materials, tools, and best practices to enhance their teaching effectiveness and adapt to evolving remote learning environments.
